title: Spaghetti Carbonara
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to boil and cook spaghetti until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.
While pasta cooks, fry pancetta in olive oil over medium heat for 5–7 minutes until crispy. Add garlic for the last 2 minutes.
Whisk together eggs, Pecorino Romano and Parmesan in a bowl. Season generously with black pepper.
Remove pan from heat. Add drained pasta to the pancetta and toss well.
Pour egg mixture over pasta, tossing quickly and adding pasta water a splash at a time to create a silky sauce. The residual heat cooks the eggs — do not put back on heat or they will scramble.
Serve immediately topped with extra cheese and cracked black pepper.

title: Chicken Tikka Masala
steps: Marinate chicken in yoghurt and tikka masala spice for at least 30 minutes (overnight is better).
Grill or pan-fry marinated chicken on high heat until charred on the outside and cooked through, about 8–10 minutes. Set aside.
In the same pan, melt butter over medium heat and sauté onion for 8 minutes until golden brown.
Add garlic and ginger and cook for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add garam masala, cumin and paprika. Stir for 1 minute to bloom the spices.
Pour in crushed tomatoes and simmer for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Blend sauce until smooth using a stick blender. Return to pan.
Stir in cream and add cooked chicken. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Season with salt, garnish with fresh coriander and serve with basmati rice or naan.

title: Beef Tacos with Mango Salsa
steps: Make mango salsa by combining mango, half the red onion, jalapeño, lime juice and coriander. Season with salt and set aside.
Brown beef mince in a hot pan over high heat, breaking up lumps, for 5–6 minutes.
Add cumin, smoked paprika, chilli powder and garlic powder. Stir well and cook 2 more minutes.
Add a splash of water and remaining red onion. Simmer for 5 minutes until sauce thickens.
Warm tortillas directly over a gas flame or in a dry pan for 30 seconds each side.
Assemble tacos: shredded cabbage first, then beef, then mango salsa and a dollop of sour cream.
Serve immediately with extra lime wedges.

title: Pan-Seared Salmon with Lemon Butter
ingredients: Fresh dill or parsley to garnish
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Pat salmon fillets completely dry with paper towel — this is key for a crispy skin. Season generously with salt, pepper and smoked paprika.
Heat olive oil in a heavy pan over high heat until almost smoking.
Place salmon skin-side down and press gently with a spatula for the first 30 seconds to prevent curling. Cook for 4 minutes without moving.
Flip salmon and cook for 2 more minutes. The salmon should be just cooked through — slightly translucent in the centre is perfect.
Reduce heat to medium. Add butter and garlic to the pan and baste salmon with the foaming butter for 1 minute.
Remove salmon. Add lemon juice and zest to the pan sauce and swirl.
Plate salmon, spoon lemon butter sauce over the top and garnish with fresh dill.

title: Classic French Omelette
ingredients: Salt and white pepper to taste
steps: Crack eggs into a bowl and beat vigorously with a fork until completely combined — no streaks of white. Season with salt and white pepper.
Heat butter in a small non-stick pan over medium-high heat. When butter foams and just starts to subside, pour in eggs.
Immediately start stirring with a rubber spatula in fast circular motions while shaking the pan. Keep the eggs moving constantly.
When eggs are just barely set — still slightly wet on top — add crème fraîche and Gruyère down the centre.
Tilt the pan at 45 degrees and roll the omelette onto itself using the spatula.
Slide onto a warm plate — it should be pale yellow with no colour on the outside. A perfect French omelette has no browning.
Garnish with chives and serve immediately.
